metadata.title: Kerr vs Schwarzschild Black Hole Comparison
metadata.description: Detailed comparison of non-rotating (Schwarzschild) and rotating (Kerr) black holes including metrics, orbital properties, shadow shapes, and astrophysical implications

schwarzschildMetric.name: Schwarzschild Solution (1916)
schwarzschildMetric.description: The unique spherically symmetric vacuum solution to Einstein's field equations. Describes a non-rotating, uncharged black hole.

metric.lineElement: ds² = -(1 - Rs/r)c²dt² + (1 - Rs/r)⁻¹dr² + r²(dθ² + sin²θ dφ²)

mass.description: The only parameter — mass completely determines the solution

killingVectors: plus two more for full SO(3)

singularity.nature: Spacelike — lies in the future of every observer inside the horizon

gravitationalMultipoles.note: Schwarzschild BH has ONLY a mass monopole — perfect spherical symmetry

kerrMetric.name: Kerr Solution (1963)
kerrMetric.description: The unique axially symmetric, stationary vacuum solution to Einstein's field equations. Describes a rotating, uncharged black hole.

where.a: a = J/(Mc) = spin parameter (angular momentum per unit mass)

metric.naturalUnits: ds² = -(1 - 2Mr/Σ)dt² - (4Mar sin²θ/Σ)dt dφ + (Σ/Δ)dr² + Σ dθ² + (r² + a² + 2Ma²r sin²θ/Σ) sin²θ dφ²
metric.note: The off-diagonal dtdφ term (frame-dragging) is the key difference from Schwarzschild

symmetries.hiddenSymmetry: Killing tensor (2nd rank) → Carter constant Q
conservedQuantities: Total: 4 constants of motion (geodesics are integrable)

singularity.location: Σ = 0, i.e., r = 0 AND θ = π/2
singularity.type: Ring singularity of radius a in the equatorial plane
singularity.note: One can pass through the ring (r = 0, θ ≠ π/2) to reach a region with r < 0

innerHorizon.name: Cauchy horizon (inner horizon)
innerHorizon.instability: Mass inflation singularity forms here in realistic collapse scenarios

horizons.horizonExistence: Requires M² ≥ a² (equivalently a/M ≤ 1). If a > M: no horizons → naked singularity (cosmic censorship forbids this).

ergosphere.polar: r_ergo = M + √(M² - a²) = r_+ (touches horizon at poles)
ergosphere.shape: Oblate — thickest at equator, zero thickness at poles
ergosphere.physics: Inside the ergosphere, no observer can remain stationary — frame-dragging forces co-rotation

determinedBy: Angular momentum J (or spin parameter a)

gravitationalMultipoles.quadrupole: Q₂ = -Ma² = -J²/M (determined by M and J alone!)
gravitationalMultipoles.allHigherMultipoles: M_l + iS_l = M(ia)^l (all multipoles determined by M and a)
gravitationalMultipoles.note: This is the essence of the no-hair theorem: ALL multipoles are determined by just M and J

properties.note: Schwarzschild is a special case of Kerr with a=0

properties.kerr: M + √(M² - a²), ranges from 2M (a=0) to M (a=M)
Exists between event horizon and stationary limit
D-shaped (flattened on approaching side) for edge-on view
Slightly smaller and asymmetric for spinning BHs
Possible in ergosphere (up to 29% of irreducible mass)
Yes — waves with ω < mΩ_H are amplified
Lower than Schwarzschild for same mass; T→0 as a→M

ISCO.description: Innermost Stable Circular Orbit — the key boundary for accretion physics

photonOrbit.description: Unstable circular photon orbit (determines the shadow boundary)

shadowComparison.description: How the shadow shape changes from Schwarzschild to Kerr

schwarzschild.shape: Perfect circle regardless of viewing angle

kerr.shape: Depends on spin parameter and viewing inclination

edgeOn_i90deg.description: Viewed from the equatorial plane — maximum asymmetry
edgeOn_i90deg.cause: Prograde photon orbits are closer to the BH, retrograde are farther

faceOn_i0deg.shape: Always circular (but slightly smaller than Schwarzschild)
faceOn_i0deg.note: Axial symmetry means the shadow is circular when viewed on-axis

sgrAstar.note: If Sgr A* has high spin and is viewed at moderate inclination, the D-shape might be detectable by ngEHT

thorneLimit.description: The maximum spin a black hole can reach through thin-disk accretion
thorneLimit.mechanism: Photons from the inner disk are preferentially captured in retrograde orbits, providing a counter-torque that prevents spin-up beyond a/M ≈ 0.998
thorneLimit.formula: The equilibrium spin depends on the radiation capture cross-section, which favors retrograde photon capture
thorneLimit.implication: Real astrophysical black holes accreting via thin disks should not exceed a/M ≈ 0.998
exceptions: BH-BH mergers can produce higher spins (up to a/M ~ 0.96 for equal-mass, aligned mergers)
MAD accretion may allow slightly different equilibrium spins
Chaotic accretion (random disk orientations) tends to spin BHs DOWN

schwarzschild.ZAMO: Zero Angular Momentum Observers are truly stationary

kerr.frameDragging: Present everywhere, strongest near the horizon
kerr.ZAMO: Zero Angular Momentum Observers are dragged with angular velocity ω = -g_tφ/g_φφ
kerr.inErgosphere: ALL observers must co-rotate — even light is dragged
kerr.atHorizon: Frame dragging angular velocity = Ω_H (horizon angular velocity)

frameDraggingVelocity.description: The angular velocity at which ZAMOs are dragged
frameDraggingVelocity.formula: ω(r,θ) = 2Mar / ((r² + a²)² - a²Δ sin²θ) (in natural units)

astrophysicalRelevance.description: Why Kerr matters more than Schwarzschild for real black holes
astrophysicalRelevance.allRealBHsSpin: Every known astrophysical process that forms a black hole imparts angular momentum. Schwarzschild (a=0) is a theoretical idealization.
whySpinMatters: Sets the ISCO radius (inner disk boundary)
Determines Hawking temperature (lower for spinning BHs)

sources: Schwarzschild, Sitzungsberichte der Preussischen Akademie der Wissenschaften, 189 (1916)
Boyer & Lindquist, Journal of Mathematical Physics 8, 265 (1967)
Chandrasekhar, 'The Mathematical Theory of Black Holes' (1983)
Visser, 'The Kerr Spacetime: A Brief Introduction' (2007)
